deep_dive.title: From Player to Master: Enhancing Test-Time Learning of LLM Agents via Reinforcement Learning over Memory
deep_dive.abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly deployed in long-running settings where improving through experience at test time becomes important. A common approach is to update an explicit memory after each interaction to guide future decisions. However, most existing methods rely on hand-designed prompting rules, making it difficult to align memory updates with downstream objectives over multi-step horizons consistently. We propose MemoPilot, a plug-in memory copilot that explicitly trains the memory update process to improve a frozen LLM's performance across sequential interactions. We formulate memory updating as a multi-turn decision problem and optimize it end-to-end with multi-turn GRPO. Our training recipe introduces (i) a turn-wise reward signal and (ii) a context-independent, turn-level advantage estimation across rollouts, enabling finer-grained credit assignment and more stable training in multi-turn settings. We evaluate MemoPilot on two testbeds: multi-round Rock-Paper-Scissors (RPS) and Limit Texas Hold'em (LHE). Across both environments, MemoPilot substantially improves test-time learning of a frozen player over strong baselines, ranking first in Elo ratings on both games (1762 on LHE and 1590 on RPS) and outperforming all baseline memory methods and proprietary models, including DeepSeek-V3.2.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Proposes MemoPilot using multi-turn GRPO reinforcement learning for memory update in LLM agents
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出 MemoPilot，使用多轮 GRPO 强化学习方法更新 LLM 代理记忆
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出 MemoPilot，使用多轮 GRPO 强化学习方法更新 LLM 代理记忆
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出 MemoPilot，使用多轮 GRPO 强化学习方法更新 LLM 代理记忆
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: MemoPilot uses reinforcement learning (GRPO) to train memory updates for LLM agents, improving test-time learning from sequential interactions.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有LLM代理在长期对话中依赖手工规则更新记忆，难以在多步交互中持续与下游目标一致。本文提出MemoPilot，将记忆更新建模为多轮决策问题，采用多轮GRPO强化学习端到端优化，无需修改冻结的LLM。在多个基准上，MemoPilot显著提升了代理的适应性和任务表现，验证了强化学习训练记忆更新的有效性，为LLM代理的持续学习提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有LLM代理在长期对话中依赖手工规则更新记忆，难以在多步交互中持续与下游目标一致。本文提出MemoPilot，将记忆更新建模为多轮决策问题，采用多轮GRPO强化学习端到端优化，无需修改冻结的LLM。在多个基准上，MemoPilot显著提升了代理的适应性和任务表现，验证了强化学习训练记忆更新的有效性，为LLM代理的持续学习提供了新思路。

deep_dive.title: Back on Track: Aligning Rewards and States for Reasoning in Diffusion Large Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) holds immense promise for enhancing the reasoning capabilities of diffusion large language models (dLLMs). However, progress is fundamentally constrained by a dual misalignment between authentic generation trajectory and the gradient update process: (i) Process-reward misalignment. Sparse, terminal rewards are indiscriminately assigned to all intermediate steps of the generation process, failing to provide discriminative credit assignment. (ii) State-trajectory misalignment. Policy updates are often diverted toward artificial, out-of-trajectory states, squandering gradients on less informative samples. To address these limitations, we introduce Process Aligned Policy Optimization (PAPO), a novel framework that holistically aligns the RL update with the dLLM's generative trajectory via Step-Aware Process Rewards (SPR) that transform sparse terminal rewards into dense, step-wise credit, and Entropy-Guided Historical Re-enactment (EHR) that replays authentic trajectories at high-uncertainty steps. Extensive experiments on four benchmarks demonstrate that PAPO significantly outperforms baselines, achieving gains of up to 4.5% on GSM8K, 4.8% on MATH500, 42.2% on Countdown and 16.1% on Sudoku.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Process Aligned Policy Optimization (PAPO) for RL in diffusion LLMs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出过程对齐策略优化(PAPO)，用于扩散大语言模型中的强化学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出过程对齐策略优化(PAPO)，用于扩散大语言模型中的强化学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出过程对齐策略优化(PAPO)，用于扩散大语言模型中的强化学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PAPO addresses process-reward and state-trajectory misalignment in diffusion LLMs via temporal credit assignment and targeted update masking.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对扩散大语言模型中强化学习的双重未对齐问题（过程奖励未对齐和状态轨迹未对齐），提出过程对齐策略优化（PAPO）。PAPO通过时序信用分配和针对性的更新掩码，确保梯度只应用于真实推导轨迹。实验表明，PAPO显著提升了推理能力，且训练稳定性优于基线。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对扩散大语言模型中强化学习的双重未对齐问题（过程奖励未对齐和状态轨迹未对齐），提出过程对齐策略优化（PAPO）。PAPO通过时序信用分配和针对性的更新掩码，确保梯度只应用于真实推导轨迹。实验表明，PAPO显著提升了推理能力，且训练稳定性优于基线。

deep_dive.title: Momentum for Reasoning: Dense Intrinsic Signals in Policy Optimization
deep_dive.abstract: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has emerged as a powerful paradigm for eliciting long-chain reasoning in large language models. However, existing methods based on Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) rely on a binary outcome reward, which induces two structural failure modes: Zero-Advantage Collapse, in which all rollouts in a group share the same outcome and the gradient vanishes, and Hallucinated Certainty, in which the model becomes increasingly confident on incorrect rollouts late in training. We address both modes by densifying the reward with intrinsic signals computed entirely from the policy's own conditional probabilities, and propose ISPO (Intrinsic Signal Policy Optimization, which combines a sequence-level signal measuring how informative the thinking trajectory is for the final answer, with a token-level directional reward whose hallucinated-certainty hinge penalizes confidently-wrong predictions at critical decision tokens. Across three base models and five mathematical reasoning benchmarks, ISPO consistently outperforms competitive baselines, with the largest gains on the hardest benchmarks where zero-advantage collapse is most frequent, and training-dynamics diagnostics confirm that both failure modes are decreased.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: reinforcement learning policy optimization with dense intrinsic signals
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 强化学习策略优化与密集内在信号
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 强化学习策略优化与密集内在信号
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 强化学习策略优化与密集内在信号
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes ISPO, a policy optimization method that densifies binary outcome rewards with intrinsic signals to fix zero-advantage collapse and hallucinated certainty in RLVR for LLM reasoning. Achieves stronger reasoning chains.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对采用二元结果奖励的强化学习可验证推理（RLVR）方法存在的梯度消失和错误自信问题，提出ISPO（内在信号策略优化），通过从策略自身条件概率中计算内在信号来密集化奖励，有效缓解了零优势崩溃和幻觉确定性两种故障模式，在长链推理任务上取得了更强的性能。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对采用二元结果奖励的强化学习可验证推理（RLVR）方法存在的梯度消失和错误自信问题，提出ISPO（内在信号策略优化），通过从策略自身条件概率中计算内在信号来密集化奖励，有效缓解了零优势崩溃和幻觉确定性两种故障模式，在长链推理任务上取得了更强的性能。

quick_skim.title: DynaCF: Mitigating Shortcut Learning in Reward Models via Dynamic Counterfactual Sensitivity
quick_skim.abstract: Reward models trained from pairwise preferences often exploit superficial shortcut cues rather than learning true response quality. We propose DynaCF, a dynamic reweighting framework for mitigating shortcut learning in reward model training. Unlike static shortcut heuristics, DynaCF measures shortcut sensitivity online during optimization by applying semantics-preserving counterfactual perturbations and tracking the resulting margin shifts and preference flips under the current model. Samples with higher shortcut sensitivity are dynamically downweighted in the Bradley-Terry objective, encouraging the model to rely less on superficial patterns and more on task-relevant preference signals. Extensive experiments show that DynaCF consistently improves robustness in preference modeling.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: RLHF reward model improvement, relevant to RL algorithms
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: RLHF奖励模型改进，与RL算法相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: RLHF奖励模型改进，与RL算法相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: RLHF奖励模型改进，与RL算法相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: DynaCF dynamically downweights shortcut-sensitive samples in reward model training using counterfactual perturbations, improving preference learning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文指出奖励模型常利用捷径线索而非真实质量，提出DynaCF框架：在线测量样本对反事实扰动的敏感性，并动态降低高敏感样本的权重，从而鼓励模型学习真实偏好信号。实验验证该方法能有效缓解捷径学习。该工作属于RLHF中的奖励模型改进，对强化学习算法有贡献。

quick_skim.title: The Art of Interrogation: Consistency Amplifies Factuality in Spatial Reasoning
quick_skim.abstract: Current Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) exhibit remarkable general capabilities but significantly underperform in spatial reasoning tasks. Existing approaches treat this gap as a knowledge deficit, relying on supervised fine-tuning (SFT) to ingest labeled spatial data from external vision sources or synthetic engines. In contrast, we argue that for many tasks, spatial reasoning capabilities are already present in pre-trained LRMs but require alignment through logical coherence under geometric 2D and 3D constraints. In this work, we propose a self-supervised reinforcement learning (RL) framework that targets the internal reasoning process without requiring ground-truth annotations. By formalizing the notion of consistency verifiers -- reward functions that check for geometric and semantic consistency under transformations -- we demonstrate that models can improve their spatial reasoning abilities. We use both image transformations, like flipping, and textual transformations, like swapping the order of objects in the question, and propose a new optimal transport-based RL strategy, OT-GRPO, which is a minimal-matching variant of group relative policy optimization tailored to pairwise verifiers. We show that this label-free consistency training approaches the accuracy of models trained with ground-truth supervision and achieves similar generalization across diverse tasks and data domains.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Self-supervised RL framework using consistency verifiers for spatial reasoning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出利用一致性验证器的自监督强化学习框架用于空间推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出利用一致性验证器的自监督强化学习框架用于空间推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出利用一致性验证器的自监督强化学习框架用于空间推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a self-supervised RL framework with consistency verifiers to align spatial reasoning in LRMs without ground-truth annotations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对大推理模型在空间推理任务上表现不佳的问题，提出一种自监督强化学习框架。该框架通过定义一致性验证器作为奖励函数，仅利用几何约束进行推理对齐，无需标注数据。实验表明，该方法显著提升了空间推理的准确性，且无需外部监督。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对大推理模型在空间推理任务上表现不佳的问题，提出一种自监督强化学习框架。该框架通过定义一致性验证器作为奖励函数，仅利用几何约束进行推理对齐，无需标注数据。实验表明，该方法显著提升了空间推理的准确性，且无需外部监督。
